books.maySubText: Free online sources.

books.title: Making of Canada: An Epic History in Twenty Extraordinary Lives
books.author: Greg Koabel
books.rationale: In this engaging book, Greg Koabel challenges the notion that pre-Confederation Canada unfolded in a linear fashion and instead suggests that nation-building involved greater "regional, national, and human diversity" than is typically acknowledged. To illustrate this argument, Koabel introduces his readers to twenty episodes significant to Canada's history but whose major players have been largely forgotten. These biographical vignettes give readers a more personal - and memorable - connection to Canadian history.
books.noteText: This book will be used over two years in high school (Rotation 2 and Rotation 3). In 2026-2027, students will read the second half of the book. Interested students may wish to read the first half over the summer, but it is not necessary since all chapters are self-contained.

books.title: A History of Canada in Ten Maps: Epic Stories of Charting a Mysterious Land
books.author: Adam Shoalts
books.rationale: This book tells the story of the discovery and development of the land that would become Canada by exploring the creation and usage of ten significant maps. It is an innovative and engaging approach to Canadian history, written by an academic and modern-day adventurer who has followed many of the routes taken by the explorers he includes in this book. It shows how history writing can create a more vivid representation of the past when it draws on biography, story-telling, geography, and cartography.
books.noteText: This book will be used over two years in high school (Rotation 2 and Rotation 3). In 2026-2027, students will read the second half of the book. Interested students may wish to read the first half over the summer, but it is not necessary since all chapters are mostly self-contained.

books.title: Early Voices
books.author: Mary Alice Downie
books.rationale: This collection of writings by 29 different women presents a kaleidoscope image of Canada's history. The wide range of individual voices, backgrounds, and histories provided by these primary documents complement the other historical non-fiction and fiction read during the year.
books.noteText: This book will be used over multiple years during high school

books.title: How Different It Was: Canadians at the Time of Confederation
books.author: Michael J. Goodspeed
books.rationale: One of the key lessons of history is that the past was - to greater or lesser degrees - fundamentally different than the present, and this is shown clearly in Michael Goodspeed's book, How Different it Was: Canadians at the Time of Confederation. Goodspeed provides a multi-faceted account of the experiences of Canadians in the 1860s, exploring factors such as geographical differences, cultural practices, family dynamics, religious observances, scientific beliefs, leisure activities, habits, and attitudes, as well as the burgeoning nation of Canada's place in the wider nineteenth-century geopolitical arena. He draws heavily on primary source material, and the book is full of historical photographs and documents.
books.noteText: E-book edition also widely available.

books.title: The Unweaving
books.author: Cheryl Perisien
books.rationale: The Red River Resistance of 1869 (also known as the First Riel Rebellion) is brought to life through the experiences of one Métis family in this work of historical fiction. Unfolding in four perspectives, this novel explores how the turmoil and the conflict affect each member of the family differently. The narrative weaves historical events and people (including the author's own ancestor) into the lives of this one family, and it is a thoroughly researched, richly detailed, and beautifully written account of the great upheaval faced by the Red River Colony in the years after Confederation.

books.title: When America First Met China

books.rationale: This is the story of trade between nations. One nation has just been born from a bloody battle with Britain, and one nation is steeped in a 2000-year tradition. books.title: A Simply Healthy Life
books.author: Caroline Fausel
books.rationale: Health coach and wellness expert Caroline Fausel introduces her readers to simple strategies for living a healthy life. From fueling our bodies and integrating exercise to using spiritual practices like Sabbath rest and setting up a system for habits, Caroline lays out the steps needed to live a healthy, fulfilled life.

books.title: Body into Balance: An Herbal Guide to Holistic Self-Care
books.author: Maria Groves
books.rationale: Herbalist Maria Noel Groves opens the door to natural wellness by taking the reader on a journey through the major body systems - respiratory, digestive, immune, nervous, memory, circulatory and more - and showing readers how to support those body systems with natural remedies.

books.title: The Joy of Movement
books.author: Kelly McGonigal
books.rationale: Kelly McGonigal, PhD, research psychologist, lecturer at Stanford University and award winning science writer, blends her passion for science and her talent for storytelling and illustrates to readers how movement is integral to both our happiness and our humanity. Readers walk away with insight from neuroscience, psychology, anthropology and biology to see how movement is intertwined with some of the most basic human joys and they learn why it is a powerful antidote to the modern epidemics of depression, anxiety and loneliness.

books.title: Eat Well and Feel Great
books.author: Tina Lond-Caulk
books.rationale: Nutritionist Tina Lond-Caulk provides teens with an easy to use evidence based handbook filled with practical tips, recipes and illustrations that make the art of eating well an achievable goal.

books.title: Paper Folding Video Lessons
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: Teaches basic paper folding techniques and helps young students gain practice in fine motor skills.

books.title: Tales from Grimm
books.author: Wanda Gag
books.rationale: Mason had her youngest students read fairy tales from Grimm or Anderson. This retelling by Wanda Gag is gentler and less scary than many other translations, while still having good literary quality and simple English for students to practice reading themselves.

books.title: Around the World in 80 Tales
books.author: Saviour Pirotta
books.rationale: This book takes readers across six continents with short, entertaining folktales to stir our first graders' imaginations with tales from many lands.

books.title: Aesop's Favorite Fables
books.author: Aesop, illus. by Milo Winter
books.rationale: Aesop's Fables have made learning life's lessons enjoyable for 2500 years. The illustrations by Milo Winter set this collection apart.
books.maySubText: another collection of Aesop's Fables. Lesson plans include the story titles.

books.title: American Tall Tales
books.author: Mary Pope Osborne
books.rationale: These larger-than-life stories capture something of America's personality. Mary Pope Osborne has retold these stories well, and the wood-carved illustrations are delightful.
books.noteText: U.S. Student Option. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: The Spirit of Canada: Canada's Story in Legends, Fiction, Poems, and Songs
books.author: Barbara Hehner
books.rationale: Organized thematically, this beautiful anthology encompasses a wide variety of voices and stories that reflect Canada's past and present. It is an excellent accompaniment to Canadian history and literature, as it contains many different genres: poems, songs, letters, legends, stories, folk tales, historical accounts, and more.
books.noteText: Canadian students should purchase this book and NOT American Tall Tales for the Tall Tales/Fables course.

Lit./Mythology: Grade 3

books.title: Charlotte's Web
books.author: E.B. White
books.rationale: The classic tale is about a spider, a pig, and much more. This book was chosen not only for its enduring literary quality but also to provide opportunities in their lessons for young readers to read books themselves.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Heart of a Samurai
books.author: Margi Preus
books.rationale: Based on the true story of a Japanese boy who survived a shipwreck and was rescued by an American whaling ship, Heart of a Samurai functions as a transitional book between Grades 3-4. Starting in Grade 4, students study world history and read literature that increasingly coordinates with the time period. Set in both Japan and the US in the mid 19th century, this engaging narrative expands students' imaginations of the broader world which they will study in more detail in later years.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: In the Days of Giants
books.author: Abbie Farwell Brown
books.rationale: The Greeks were not the only ones with a story tradition that influenced Western culture. These Norse stories have also been highly influential, and Brown has retold them beautifully.

books.title: Russian Fairy Tales
books.author: Alexander Afanasyev
books.rationale: Pushkin, Afanasyev, and many others wrote and collected Russian folk tales in the 19th century. Students read a few tales in the lesson plans and may enjoy the others on their own time.
books.noteText: Although the free and hard copy editions are not identical, they are from the same translator with nearly identical language. One tale scheduled in the lesson plans is in only the free text and the second is in both versions.
books.sharedText:
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6

books.title: South American Jungle Tales
books.author: Horacio Quiroga
books.rationale: A Uruguayan author and playwright who lived at the end of the 19th century, Quiroga is sometimes compared to Rudyard Kipling and Edgar Allen Poe.
books.noteText: Students only read a tale or two from this collection, so the free version is recommended.
books.sharedText:
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6

books.title: Short Stories & Folktales
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans curate and include additional folk tales and shorter writings from 19th century authors such as Tolstoy and George Macdonald.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4

books.title: The Tales of Uncle Remus: The Adventures of Brer Rabbit
books.author: Julius Lester
books.rationale: Whether he is besting Brer Fox or sneaking into Mr. Man's garden, Brer Rabbit demonstrates that brains are better than brawn, along with other life lessons. These classic tales from the African American tradition are full of humor and creativity, and Julius Lester's retelling makes them a delight for readers today.

books.title: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
books.author: C. S. Lewis
books.rationale: This piece of classic literature by C.S. Lewis was chosen to foster students' moral formation and love for a well-told story.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.maySubText: another in the Narnia series or a title in adjacent grades if students have already read The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe

books.title: Hans Brinker or The Silver Skates
books.author: Mary Mapes Dodge
books.rationale: Mary Mapes Dodge was recognized as one of the leading authors of children's literature during the 19th century, and Hans Brinker is her most famous work. With themes of perseverance and selflessness, Hans Brinker's heartwarming tale of two siblings delights, instructs, and expands the imagination.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grade 4
General Lit.: Grades 5-6

books.title: The Age of Fable
books.author: Thomas Bulfinch
books.rationale: The Age of Fable contains mythological stories primarily from Greece and Rome, although it also includes some stories from Norse and Eastern traditions. This is a stretch book for Grades 5-6 as the reading level is more challenging than the mythology stories assigned in the earlier grades.

books.title: A Christmas Carol
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.rationale: As a famous work of 19th century literature, A Christmas Carol expands students' understanding of this historical period while also nourishing them with a thought-provoking tale about greed and generosity. It is scheduled in the mythology stream as Dicken's work has become a modern myth, shaping our conceptions of Christmas and more.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Little Women
books.author: Louisa May Alcott
books.rationale: A beloved piece of 19th century literature, this story follows four sisters as they grow and learn to live full and happy lives. The story is loosely set during the American Civil War and has remained popular since it was first published.

books.title: The Lightning Dreamer: Cuba's Greatest Abolitionist
books.author: Margarita Engle
books.rationale: This book is a poetic fictionalized account of famous Cuban abolitionist and author Gertrudis Gómez Avellaneda (1814-1873). Since Avellaneda wrote in Spanish, this biography allows students to get a glimpse of a significant 19th century author outside the English canon. Engle's style also provides opportunities for students to consider the differences between poetry and prose and experiment with point of view in connected composition assignments.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Little Women
books.author: Louisa May Alcott
books.rationale: A beloved piece of 19th century literature, this story follows four sisters as they grow and learn to live full and happy lives. The story is loosely set during the American Civil War and has remained popular since it was first published.

Grades 7-8: Choose this OR Sir Gibbie.

General Lit: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 5-6

books.title: Sir Gibbie
books.author: George MacDonald
books.rationale: Although less well known than some of his friends, George Macdonald cast an outsized influence on his contemporaries and many 20th century writers who followed him, including C.S. Lewis and J.R.R. Tolkein. Macdonald's genius for portraying goodness and inspiring readers is on full display in this rags-to-riches tale of a mute boy from the Scotch Highlands.
books.noteText: Choose this OR Little Women.

books.title: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass
books.author: Frederick Douglass
books.rationale: Students read extended selections of this classic 19th century narrative which details the author's story from slavery to freedom. For US students, this text will enhance their reading of Lincoln & Douglass in US History.
books.noteText: GRADES 9+ should choose this OR Up From Slavery and Souls of Black Folk.
books.sharedText:
General Lit: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.rationale: A famous gothic novel first published in 1818 and then revised in 1831, Frankenstein influenced the development of science fiction. This gripping story was composed as a result of a writing challenge when the author was only 19, and it revolves around personhood, conscience, and the ethics of scientific ambition, themes which are especially important for students to wrestle with in today's age of AI.

books.title: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass
books.author: Frederick Douglass
books.rationale: Students read extended selections of this classic 19th century narrative which details the author's story from slavery to freedom. For US students, this text will enhance their reading of Lincoln & Douglass in US History.

General Lit.: Grades 9-10

books.title: The Souls of Black Folk
books.author: W.E.B. Dubois
books.rationale: This landmark text of American literature and sociology is paired with Up from Slavery by Booker T. Washington in the lesson plans. This pairing immerses students in the dialogues around the turn of the century as they read about Washington's life and aspirations and then consider Dubois's response.
books.noteText: Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Up from Slavery
books.author: Booker T. Washington
books.rationale: This is a first-person account of what it was like to be born on a slave plantation, live through the Civil War and Reconstruction, and make a new life as a free person. Washington's accomplishments and sense of hope are inspiring, and his founding of the Tuskegee Institute which continues as a historically black university and National Historic Site today is a remarkable achievement. Lesson plans pair Dubois's response to Washington to allow students a richer experience of the discussions of the times.
books.noteText:
Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.

Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Silas Marner
books.author: George Eliot (Mary Ann Evans)
books.rationale: Students spend two terms lingering with this nineteenth-century novel which depicts British village life while stretching the reader's moral imagination. Composition lessons build on these lessons by asking students to analyze characters, compare characters’ development during the two time periods of the novel, and use the events of the novel to discuss ethical questions.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: English Literature for Boys and Girls
books.author: H.E. Marshall
books.rationale: A delightful introduction to the writers of English literature whose works hold the greatest appeal for the youthful reader. The life and personality of each author is given in outline, with enough material quoted from key works to give an idea of the text.
books.noteText: Selections used across multiple rotations.

books.title: History of Literature PDF
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: Curated selections of readings on literary developments, significant authors, and primary sources from the 19th century. Students use this PDF along with selected links and readings in Marshall's and Long's spines to deepen their relationship with 19th-century literature.

books.title: The Tempest
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: This play is often called a Tragicomedy because it has both comedic and tragic elements. It is about an overthrown Duke and his daughter who are exiled on an island. The play addresses the themes of power, betrayal, magic, slavery, and the restoration of relationships.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.author: Arkangel
books.maySubText: Librivox recording

Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: Henry IV Part 2
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: This history play is a tale about Prince Hal falling into his father's footsteps as king. Hal's life will no longer be the same when he feels the weight of the crown.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Winter's Tale
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: The Winter's Tale is play divided into two halves—tragedy and redemption. It begins in the court of Leontes, King of Sicilia, whose sudden and irrational jealousy leads him to accuse his wife Hermione of betrayal. His actions cause devastating consequences, including the loss of family, friendship, and trust. This play emphasizes the power of jealousy to destroy, but the greater power of love to restore.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.author: Eavan Boland, Mark Strand
books.rationale: Students read this anthology to learn how to study the characteristics and history of various poetic forms and then enjoy a number of examples from poets across the years, including many from this year's time period.

books.title: The Word Made Fresh
books.author: Abram Van Engen
books.rationale: Van Engen lays out a beautiful relational approach to poetry which resonates deeply with Mason. As he models a love for poetry and gives insights into how to enjoy, understand, and read poetry (or start on the journey!), students will continue growing their own delight. Lesson plans pair readings from the book with practical exercises.
books.noteText: The first section of the book (the more practical part) is scheduled in the lesson plans, but we recommend the whole book - which concludes with a theological reflection on the value of poetry - as a resource for parents and teachers.

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas, translated and edited by Lowell Bair
books.rationale: A sweeping tale of (in)justice and revenge inspired by a real-life case, this historical adventure novel became one of Alexandre Dumas's most popular works.
books.noteText: Many different editions of this book exist, so if buying used be sure you are purchasing the Bantam Classics edition translated and abridged by Lowell Bair. Bair's version shortens the original work to a (somewhat) more manageable length without truncating the richness of the language and plot. If interested in reading the full text, we recommend Robin Buss's translation published by Penguin.

books.title: Jane Eyre
books.author: Charlotte Brontë, edited by Karen Swallow Prior
books.rationale: A coming of age story narrated in first person and set in 19th century England. Students experience the world through Jane's eyes and wrestle along with her when she faces various events and quandaries.
books.noteText:
This edition includes the full text along with reflection questions and an introduction. Since the lesson plans refer to this additional content, students should use this edition if possible.  Due to it's length, students have the option to finish this book on their own time after end of term.

Choose Jane Eyre OR James. Students more interested in American Lit should consider James. Those more interested in British Lit should consider Jane Eyre.

books.title: James
books.author: Percival Everett
books.rationale: Recently published and already widely acclaimed, James retells Twain's Adventures of Huckleberry Finn from Jim's point of view. Funny and intense by turns and always insightful, Everett immerses students in 19th-century America. Alveary lessons pair selections of Huckleberry Finn alongside James to enrich students' understanding of the texts and times.
books.noteText:
Only recommended for mature 11-12th grade students. Due to it's length, students have the option to finish this book on their own time after end of term.

Choose James OR Jane Eyre. Students more interested in American Lit should consider James. Those more interested in British Lit should consider Jane Eyre.

books.title: Short stories and other writings
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans curate a selection of writings from a variety of 19th century authors such as Pushkin, O.Henry, Tagore, and others to give students exposure to a broader range of genres, authors, and ideas.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Things Fall Apart
books.author: Chinua Achebe
books.rationale: Widely considered to be the foundational text of modern African fiction, Things Fall Apart provides a detailed, unflinching depiction of the complex life and beliefs of the Igbo tribe of Nigeria in the late 1800s, before and after their first encounters with European missionaries and the establishment of a colonial government. The story traces the life of the warrior Okonkwo: his upbringing and adulthood, his rise to prominence and and tragic fall, and finally his banishment and return to find his home forever changed. Written in English from an authentically African perspective, Achebe's novel directly challenges Western narratives of colonialism and stereotypes of Africa, yet eschews any simple resolutions.
books.noteText: Recommended for mature Grades 11-12 students due to sensitive subject matter. Students who might need a lighter read may choose a title from the Grades 9-10 literature selections.

books.title: English Literature for Boys and Girls
books.author: H.E. Marshall
books.rationale: A delightful introduction to the writers of English literature whose works hold the greatest appeal for the youthful reader. The life and personality of each author is given in outline, with enough material quoted from key works to give an idea of the text.
books.noteText: Selections used across multiple rotations.

books.title: The Word Made Fresh
books.author: Abram Van Engen
books.rationale: Van Engen lays out a beautiful relational approach to poetry which resonates deeply with Mason. As he models a love for poetry and gives insights into how to enjoy, understand, and read poetry (or start on the journey!), students will continue growing their own delight. Lesson plans pair readings from the book with practical exercises.
books.noteText: The first section of the book (the more practical part) is scheduled in the lesson plans, but we recommend the whole book - which concludes with a theological reflection on the value of poetry - as a resource for parents and teachers.

Poetry Study: Grade 10

books.title: Poetry Anthology
books.author: Various

books.title: The Lightning Dreamer: Cuba's Greatest Abolitionist
books.author: Margarita Engle
books.rationale: This book is a poetic fictionalized account of famous Cuban abolitionist and author Gertrudis Gómez Avellaneda (1814-1873). Since Avellaneda wrote in Spanish, this biography allows students to get a glimpse of a significant 19th century author outside the English canon. Engle's style also provides opportunities for students to consider the differences between poetry and prose and experiment with point of view in connected composition assignments.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.author: Frederick Douglass
books.rationale: Students read extended selections of this classic 19th century narrative which details the author's story from slavery to freedom. For US students, this text will enhance their reading of Lincoln & Douglass in US History.
books.noteText: GRADES 9+ should choose this OR Up From Slavery and Souls of Black Folk.
books.sharedText:
General Lit: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: The Souls of Black Folk
books.author: W.E.B. Dubois
books.rationale: This landmark text of American literature and sociology is paired with Up from Slavery by Booker T. Washington in the lesson plans. This pairing immerses students in the dialogues around the turn of the century as they read about Washington's life and aspirations and then consider Dubois's response.
books.noteText: Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Up from Slavery
books.author: Booker T. Washington
books.rationale: This is a first-person account of what it was like to be born on a slave plantation, live through the Civil War and Reconstruction, and make a new life as a free person. Washington's accomplishments and sense of hope are inspiring, and his founding of the Tuskegee Institute which continues as a historically black university and National Historic Site today is a remarkable achievement. Lesson plans pair Dubois's response to Washington to allow students a richer experience of the discussions of the times.
books.noteText:
Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.

Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas, translated and edited by Lowell Bair
books.rationale: A sweeping tale of (in)justice and revenge inspired by a real-life case, this historical adventure novel became one of Alexandre Dumas's most popular works.
books.noteText: Many different editions of this book exist, so if buying used be sure you are purchasing the Bantam Classics edition translated and abridged by Lowell Bair. Bair's version shortens the original work to a (somewhat) more manageable length without truncating the richness of the language and plot. If interested in reading the full text, we recommend Robin Buss's translation published by Penguin.

books.title: Jane Eyre
books.author: Charlotte Brontë, edited by Karen Swallow Prior
books.rationale: A coming of age story narrated in first person and set in 19th century England. Students experience the world through Jane's eyes and wrestle along with her when she faces various events and quandaries.
books.noteText:
This edition includes the full text along with reflection questions and an introduction. Since the lesson plans refer to this additional content, students should use this edition if possible.  Due to it's length, students have the option to finish this book on their own time after end of term.

books.title: Denison Pre-Algebra
books.author: Denison Algebra
books.rationale: Denison offers a complete high school math program with video lessons taught by a veteran teacher and homeschool dad for Pre-Algebra through Algebra 2. Pre-algebra only requires familiarity with the four basic functions (add, subtract, multiply, and divide). After Pre-Algebra, the course splits into two tracks for Algebra, Geometry, and Algebra 2: a track for students who don't have significant struggles but aren't enjoying math, and a Success track for students who struggle with math and need shorter lessons with more scaffolding.
books.noteText:
Choose this or Hands-On Equations
If following the Denison track, you only need to purchase this text for 7th-grade math. Use this in place of Hands-On Equations AND RightStart Geometry.

books.title: Over and Under the Pond
books.author: Kate Messner
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes us on a trip to the pond alongside mother and son. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about pond habitats as preferred by the teacher.

books.title: Bat Loves the Night
books.author: Nicola Davies
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book illuminates the life of a common but less-often-observed creature. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about bats as preferred by the teacher.

books.title: Winter Sleep: A Hibernation Story
books.author: Sean Taylor
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes readers on a winter walk with a boy and his grandmother. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about a winter habitat preferred by the teacher.

books.title: A Fruit is a Suitcase for Seeds
books.author: Jean Richards
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to the ways that plants use seeds. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about seeds as preferred by the teacher.

books.title: Up in the Garden and Down in the Dirt
books.author: Kate Messner
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes us on a trip to the garden alongside a girl and her grandmother. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about garden habitats as preferred by the teacher.

books.title: A House Spider's Life
books.author: John Himmelman
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book illuminates the life of a common, but often less appreciated, creature. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book about spiders as preferred by the teacher.

books.title: Walks with a Naturalist: Living Nature Reader Volume 1
books.author: Melissa Dowland
books.rationale: Our nature reader brings learners alongside an experienced nature educator as she relates her stories with curiosity, delight, and humility.  Volume 1 explores insects, spiders, birds, seeds, and more.
books.noteText: The print book is formatted for easy reading; the eBook is formatted with enhanced typesetting (i.e. the print automatically adjusts to the device used to read it and the reader can adjust fonts, colors, etc).
books.maySubText: another nature reader that provides accurate, living ideas across a variety of creatures; supplemental links and prompts may not apply to substituted reader

books.title: A Walk in the...
books.author: Rebecca L. Johnson
books.rationale: These beautiful picture books take us on a walk through the biomes, introducing the various plants and animals we might encounter in various seasons. They are a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.noteText:
Links provide various options in this biome book series. Teachers should select one that is familiar to the student for Term 1 and one that will be less familiar for Term 3.

Deciduous Forest is available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about a biome (one that is familiar for Term 1; one that is not for Term 3).

books.title: Monarch and Milkweed
books.author: Helen Frost
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to an insect and its relationship with the environment. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about an insect within the context of its plant community.

books.title: Desert Giant
books.author: Barbara Bash
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to the saguaro cactus, a keystone species in the desert biome. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about a keystone species within the context of its community.

books.title: Mama Built a Little Nest
books.author: Jennifer Ward
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to the variety of ways that birds build homes for their offspring. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about birds and their different types of nests.

books.title: The Secret Life of the Skunk
books.author: Laurence Pringle
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book illuminates the life of a common but less-often-observed creature.  It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about a mammal

books.title: Walks with a Naturalist: Living Nature Reader Volume 2
books.author: Melissa Dowland
books.rationale: Our nature reader brings learners alongside an experienced nature educator as she relates her stories with curiosity, delight, and humility.  Volume 2 explores insects, insect larvae, mammals, some aquatic species, and more.
books.noteText: The print book is formatted for easy reading; the eBook is formatted with enhanced typesetting (i.e. the print automatically adjusts to the device used to read it and the reader can adjust fonts, colors, etc).
books.maySubText: another nature reader that provides accurate, living ideas across a variety of creatures; supplemental links and prompts may not apply to substituted reader

books.title: Buzztail and Leaper
books.author: Robert M. McClung
books.rationale: Two stories in one, this classic presents two very different animals in the context of their ecosystems. Students read about their habitats, biomes, food chains, life cycles, adaptations, and more.

books.title: Next Time You See a Cloud
books.author: Emily Morgan
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book calls attention to physical features in the larger ecosystem: weather changes and the movement of water. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: Going Home
books.author: Marianne Berkes
books.rationale: This lovely picture book introduces students to animal migration as a global phenomenon.  It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.
books.maySubText: any life-giving picture book preferred by the teacher that is about animal migration.

books.title: Christian Liberty Nature Reader Book 4
books.author: Edward J. Shewan
books.rationale: Introduces 21st-century North American students to a variety of common birds, insects, reptiles, and their habits.

books.title: The Universe in You: A Microscopic Journey
books.author: Jason Chin
books.rationale: This stunning picture book introduces students to Things-too-small-to-see.  With beautiful artwork and accessible language, this title is sure to inspire everyone to look more closely.

books.title: To Fly: The Story of the Wright Brothers
books.author: Wendie Old
books.rationale: This book tells the story of an important technology that students have probably observed and maybe even used. Along the way, they learn how the Wright Brothers were driven by curiosity and inspiration and how they persisted to solve one of the biggest challenges of their day.
books.maySubText: First to Fly by Peter Busby

books.title: The Elephant Scientist
books.author: Caitlin O'Connell and Donna M. Jackson
books.rationale: Challenges arise when wild animals meet human communities. The scientist in this story not only observes elephants, but she also uses what she learns from them, along with what she already knows about sound waves, to collaborate with the local community to navigate such challenges.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: A World in a Drop of Water: Exploring with a Microscope
books.author: Alvin and Virginia Silverstein
books.rationale: This book introduces students to the microscopic creatures that live in natural water. They will examine pond water under a microscope to see the hidden lives of single-celled organisms.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

books.title: The Octopus Scientists: Exploring the Mind of a Mollusk
books.author: Sy Montgomery
books.rationale: This book provides students with a closer look at a creature from the family Mollusca. With respect and persistence, the author pursues the mysterious and fascinating octopus - and shares all that she learns with us, of course!
books.noteText: Available on Audible and Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: Life on Surtsey: Iceland's Upstart Island
books.author: Loree Griffin Burns
books.rationale: In 1963, a volcano erupted and created a new island off the coast of Iceland. This book tells the story of the scientists who are watching and recording life unfold through primary succession.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

books.title: Science: Grade 4 Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 4, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The teacher's primary role is teaching students to follow directions.
books.noteText:
Relevant supplies from the Grade 4 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book.  It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term. 

NOTE: Also included in Grade 4 Bundles with Alveary Printing store.

books.title: The Hive Detectives: Chronicle of a Honey Bee Catastrophe
books.author: Loree Griffin Burns
books.rationale: This book shows concepts from ecology, mathematics, and chemistry as it invites students into conversation on an important current issue.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with advanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: The Tornado Scientist
books.author: Mary Kay Carson
books.rationale: In this captivating story, readers follow a tornado scientist, learn about her life, her work, and her passion to save lives by improving our understanding of the weather.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: Mission to Pluto: The First Visit to an Ice Dwarf and the Kuiper Belt
books.author: Mary Kay Carson
books.rationale: Students learn about the discovery of Pluto and join the scientists who are learning about our solar system. How do we learn about very big things and those that are very far away? We find out on a Mission to Pluto!
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

books.title: Nature All Around: Plants
books.author: Pamela Hickman
books.rationale: We have chosen this lovely companion to our picture book selection for our 5th graders' introduction to botany. This title uses simple language, introduces new terms, and incorporates artwork that is both beautiful and useful for students who are ready to get to know the world of plants more closely!

books.title: The Forest in the Tree
books.author: Ailsa Wild, Aviva Reed, Briony Barr, & Gregory Crocetti
books.rationale: This lovely picture book brings together important botanical and ecological topics within the context of a story.

books.title: Shanleya's Quest Card Game
books.author: Thomas Elpel
books.rationale: Botany in a Day author, Thomas Elpel, produced this deck of cards to help students begin to notice the patterns that are used to characterize common plant families.
books.noteText:
For Grade 5, this resource is not required but is highly recommended for optional activities included in the lesson plans.
For the Botany Elective in HS, this resource is REQUIRED.
books.sharedText:
Natural History: Grade 5
Botany Lessons

books.title: The Bat Scientists
books.author: Mary Kay Carson
books.rationale: This book provides students with a closer look at an endangered species that might be right in their neighborhood. Follow Dr. Tuttle in his tireless work to understand and protect the bat and to maintain balance in the ecosystem.

books.title: Beetle Busters
books.author: Loree Griffin Burns
books.rationale: The Asian longhorned beetle has devastated forest ecosystems in America. Students are introduced to basic forestry as they learn about the problem of invasive species. The author beautifully invites the reader to consider the challenges of dealing with this complex issue and encourages dialogue and collaboration as part of the problem-solving process.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: Science: Grade 5 Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 5, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The teacher's primary role is teaching students to follow directions.
books.noteText:
Relevant supplies from the Grade 5 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book. It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term.  

NOTE: Also included in Grade 5 Bundles with Alveary Printing store.

books.title: Plate Tectonics
books.author: Fiona Young-Brown
books.rationale: This text explores the field of plate tectonics from its early history to the current era.  Students will consider how such theories explain the natural phenomena that have contributed to the Earth's development, as well as events such as volcanoes and earthquakes.

books.title: Phineas Gage
books.author: John Fleischman
books.rationale: In this classic story, readers hear the story of a man who suffers an horrific railroad accident.  The resulting brain injury forever changes his life and produces one the most fascinating case studies that neuroscience has ever seen. Note that the author associates social ability and success with human value. Discuss with students as appropriate.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: The Art of Construction
books.author: Mario Salvadori
books.rationale: We love that this book provides hands-on activities for exploring the principles of engineering, allowing students to dip their toes into the engineering process with everyday materials.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

books.title: Tracking Trash
books.author: Loree Griffin Burns
books.rationale: Students follow a scientist who uses creative methods to better understand ocean currents and our impact on life within the sea.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: The Wolves and Moose of Isle Royale: Restoring an Island Ecosystem
books.author: Nancy F. Castaldo
books.rationale: Students discover the threatened predator-prey relationship that maintains the Isle Royale ecosystem in this fascinating story. Will scientists be able to restore the balance?  Let's follow this living laboratory experiment.

books.title: The Frog Scientist
books.author: Pamela Turner
books.rationale: Scientists now know that frogs are particularly sensitive to pollution. Readers join the work of scientists who are learning just how chemical run-off affects their life cycle. This text is a particularly good example of the relationship between field and laboratory work and introduces important facets of excellent experimental design "by-the-way."
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: Science: Grade 6 Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 6, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The teacher's primary role is teaching students to follow directions.
books.noteText:
Relevant supplies from the Grade 6 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book. It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term.  

NOTE: Also included in Grade 6 Bundles with Alveary Printing store.

books.title: The Story of Science: Aristotle Leads the Way
books.author: Joy Hakim
books.rationale: This series situates facts and theories within their historical, political, and cultural context.  The author tells the story of (physical) science as a process of developing knowledge that is intimately tied to human society. Ms. Hakim challenges readers to consider differing viewpoints, so as to understand science with uncommon wholeness.
books.noteText:
Sensitive content. Lesson plans provide support, but teachers might choose to pre-read and plan how to handle potentially controversial topics.

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: The Girl Who Drew Butterflies: How Maria Merian's Art Changed Science
books.author: Joyce Sidman
books.rationale: One of the first naturalists to observe live insects directly, Maria Sibylla Merian was also one of the first to document the metamorphosis of the butterfly and the close relationships between insects and plants. In this richly illustrated nonfiction biography, readers meet the "mother" of ecology, a woman who flouted convention in the pursuit of knowledge.
books.noteText: Available on Audible and Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: Science: Grade 7 Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 7, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The lab book builds upon those from Form 2 (Grades 4-6) but also supports those new to the program in Form 3 (Grades 7-8). It has been designed for a gentler transition to high school science, as well.
books.noteText:
Relevant supplies from the Grade 7 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book. It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term.  

NOTE: Also included in Grade 7 Bundles with Alveary Printing store.

books.author: Joy Hakim
books.rationale: This series situates facts and theories within their historical, political, and cultural context.  The author tells the story of (physical) science as a process of developing knowledge that is intimately tied to human society. Ms. Hakim challenges readers to consider differing viewpoints, so as to understand science with uncommon wholeness.
books.noteText:
Sensitive content. Lesson plans provide support, but teachers might choose to pre-read and plan how to handle potentially controversial topics.

books.title: The Story of Science: Einstein Adds a New Dimension
books.author: Joy Hakim
books.rationale: This series situates facts and theories within their historical, political, and cultural context.  The author tells the story of (physical) science as a process of developing knowledge that is intimately tied to human society. Ms. Hakim challenges readers to consider differing viewpoints, so as to understand science with uncommon wholeness.
books.noteText:
Sensitive content. Lesson plans provide support, but teachers might choose to pre-read and plan how to handle potentially controversial topics.

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: Invincible Microbe
books.author: Jim Murphy
books.rationale: Murphy tells the story of an ancient and elusive microbe, weaving together ideas from science, history, and ethics. This author is a master storyteller, and we can't imagine a better way to continue our journey through natural history and into the very timely topic of microbiology and infectious disease.

books.title: Science: Grade 8 Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 8, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The lab book is designed to provide a foundation for the high school disciplines.
books.noteText:
Relevant supplies from the Grade 8 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book. It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term.  

NOTE: Also included in Grade 8 Bundles with Alveary Printing store.

books.title: HS Chem Intro
books.author: Danielle Merritt-Sunseri
books.noteText: Watch this introduction to glimpse our video companion lessons that accompany High School Chemistry.

books.title: Michael Faraday's The Chemical History of a Candle
books.author: Bill Hammack & Don DeCoste
books.rationale: This book introduces modern readers to Michael Faraday’s great nineteenth-century lectures on "The Chemical History of a Candle." Students will be watching the lectures on video, so this text is for teachers or for students who would like to follow along using the text.

books.title: Introductory Chemistry: A Foundation
books.author: Steven S. Zumdahl & Donald J. DeCoste
books.rationale: Masters at inspiring and engaging students, the authors of this best-selling text combine problem-solving strategies, early coverage of chemical reactions, accessible explanations and visualizations, and an emphasis on everyday applications to facilitate understanding.
books.noteText: Available in print or eBook with enhanced typesetting and audio. Previous edition is acceptable.

books.title: The Chemical Elements Coloring and Activity Book
books.author: Ellen Johnston McHenry
books.rationale: All 118 elements are included in coloring pages, with information about its discovery, examples of molecules it will form, and pictures of how the element might be used in industry, medicine, the military, research, and fine arts.  Also included are a personalizable periodic table and a variety of games.
books.maySubText: Teachers may substitute any periodic table game/resource that they prefer, such as the board game Periodic or Theodore Gray's The Elements.

books.title: Alveary Chemistry Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all of the instructions, procedures, and guidance needed to complete the laboratory component of High School Chemistry.
books.noteText: Relevant supplies from the Grade 9 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book. It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term.

books.title: The Biology Coloring Book
books.author: Robert D. Griffin
books.rationale: In The Biology Coloring Book, students learn about the basic processes of life and the interactions of living things within their environment.
books.maySubText: Teachers may substitute any games or activities on the topics covered in the General Biology text.

books.title: General Biology, 2nd Edition
books.author: Heather Ayala & Katie Rogstad
books.rationale: In a conversational tone and with reverence, the authors of this general biology text clearly guide students from the molecules of biology to the complexities of the human body and evolution.

books.title: The Fool and the Heretic
books.author: Todd Wood & Darrel Falk
books.rationale: Two scientists with differing perspectives dialogue respectfully about creation and evolution.  This is an excellent choice for students in contexts that perceive faith and science to be antagonistic, as well as those interested in learning to have healthy discussion with those of differing viewpoints.
books.noteText:
Choose this OR The Spirit of the Rainforest.
Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.
books.maySubText: another selection from the Alveary Bookshelf.

books.title: The Spirit of the Rainforest
books.author: Dr. Rosa Vasquez Espinoza
books.rationale: A leading scientist explores the synergy between indigenous knowledge and classical science in this courageous and deeply spiritual adventure. This is an excellent choice for students who are secure in the harmony between faith and science and would benefit from a living example in their modern world, as well as those interested in indigenous voices.
books.noteText:
Choose this OR The Fool and the Heretic.
Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.
books.maySubText: another selection from the Alveary Bookshelf.

books.title: Alveary Biology Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all of the instructions, procedures, and guidance needed to complete the laboratory component of High School Biology.
books.noteText: Relevant supplies from the Grade 10 list are further detailed in the Table of Contents for the Lab Book. It is recommended that supplies are obtained either for the year or by term.

books.author: Eugene Hecht, PhD
books.rationale: This book is listed as a resource/supplement to the Math with Borders Physics video course.

books.title: Home Study Companion: Physics
books.author: Math Without Borders
books.rationale: This living lecture series teaches the basic concepts of physics by guiding students to mathematically analyze moving objects in the world around them.

books.title: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat: Mastering the Elements of Good Cooking
books.author: Samin Nosrat
books.rationale: Whether for afternoon occupations or High School Food Science, young cooks will be inspired by this well-written text with its engaging narrative and beautiful illustrations.  The author does a good job of introducing the basic ingredients of cooking without requiring a full chemistry course; students who have already taken chemistry will find additional connections to enhance their understanding even more.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.title: The Elements of Baking
books.author: Katarina Cermelj
books.rationale: This title is ideal for learning the science of baking, in which students will learn about and observe the properties of their ingredients, experiment with substitutions, and work toward their desired product.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting.

books.title: The Things We Make
books.author: Bill Hammack, Ph.D
books.rationale: From medieval cathedrals to modern soda cans, this book will help learners grasp 'the engineering method' and notice it working all around them.

books.rationale: This well-written and humorously illustrated classic guides readers through the fundamental principles of machines and shows how the developments of the past are building blocks of the world of tomorrow.

books.title: By Design: Ethics, Theology, and the Practice of Engineering

books.rationale: An often overlooked topic, this text challenges students to consider the social context and formative nature of design.

books.title: What a Plant Knows

books.rationale: A plant knows more than you think! This piece of botany literature provides a fascinating look at plants.

books.title: The Milkweed Lands: An Epic Story of One Plant: Its Nature and Ecology
books.author: Eric Lee-Mader
books.rationale: Delve into the fascinating role of this often-overlooked plant in a wide variety of ecosystems.  A collaboration between a noted ecologist and an award-winning botanical illustrator, this text will inspire readers in their own special studies.

books.title: Shanleya's Quest Card Game

books.noteText: Annotations and updates to text included with lesson plans.

books.author: Peter Wohlleben
books.rationale: This title provides students with current scientific information and human connections to support their knowledge about trees both as individuals and within their context.  The beautiful photography in this piece of botany literature is inspiring and supportive.

books.title: The Sibley Guide to Trees
books.author: David Allen Sibley
books.rationale: Botany students use this guide to study tree families from across North America and to consider various aspects of tree anatomy.

books.title: Oaxaca Journal
books.author: Oliver Sacks
books.rationale: A master writer takes you on a trip to discover ferns in southern Mexico in this work of botany literature.

books.title: Master of Field Arts
books.author: Roseann Hanson
books.rationale: Welcome to the next level for nature journalers and field sketchers—a deep dive into becoming a dedicated master naturalist and field artist.

books.title: The Field Guide to Geology
books.author: David Lambert
books.rationale: Basic principles of geology are laid out well with helpful illustrations.

books.title: Roadside Geology/Geology Underfoot Series
books.author: various

books.title: Annals of the Former World
books.author: John McPhee
books.rationale: Like the terrain it covers, Annals of the Former World tells a multilayered tale, and the reader may choose one of many paths through it. This is a long book, which students may or may not finish, depending on interest.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: The Map That Changed the World
books.author: Simon Winchester
books.rationale: A biography of a famous geologist.  This optional selection can be added to enhance the literary component of the course.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting, Audible, and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: 101 American Geo-Sites You've Gotta See
books.author: Albert Dickas
books.rationale: Geographical survey of geological sites of interest, includes a well-written geological analysis on each site and excellent coverage across regions of the U.S.  Canadian learners may prefer Aerial Geology.

books.author: Donald R. Prothero
books.rationale: A fascinating look at what the rocks tell us according to a well-known expert in his field.

books.rationale: A conversational guide through Astronomy Field Studies

books.author: Kober, Benecchi, & Gossard

books.title: Astronomy Textbook
books.author: Charlesworth, Castro, & Delgado

books.title: The Last Stargazers
books.author: Emily Levesque
books.rationale: From the lonely quiet of midnight stargazing to tall tales of wild bears loose in the observatory, The Last Stargazers is a love letter to astronomy and an affirmation of the crucial role that humans can and must play in the future of scientific discovery.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.title: The Day Without Yesterday
books.author: John Farrell
